skill.name: revise
skill.description: Revise the most recent or provided p11 shareable document based on comments/replies and publish a new version. Use when Codex should apply clear reviewer decisions to a p11 document.
skill.summary: Applies reviewer comments to p11 documents and publishes updated versions.
skill.author: rarexlabs

security_audit.summary: All 57 static findings are false positives. Weak cryptographic algorithm flags were triggered by a hex color code ("#2563EB" in openai.yaml:3), a React component name ("DefinitionDescription" in components.md:34), and YAML metadata fields (SKILL.md:3-5). Ruby/shell backtick execution flags were triggered by Markdown code block backticks in code fences across SKILL.md and components.md. The system reconnaissance flag on SKILL.md:92 is actually security-conscious guidance about bearer credentials. The skill has legitimate external command usage via the p11 CLI, which is expected and necessary for its document revision workflow. No malicious intent detected.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Detection
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged hex color code '#2563EB' (openai.yaml:3), component name 'DefinitionDescription' (components.md:34), and YAML description fields (SKILL.md:3-5) as weak cryptographic algorithms. None of these are cryptographic material. Hex color codes and component names are false positives for crypto detection patterns.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks as Ruby Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Static scanner misidentified Markdown code fence backticks as Ruby shell backtick execution. All 52 flagged instances across SKILL.md and components.md are standard Markdown code block formatting (triple ``` or inline ` backticks) for displaying code or bash command examples, not shell command execution.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged SKILL.md:92 as system reconnaissance. This line instructs the AI to 'Treat edit URLs as bearer credentials and avoid exposing them unnecessarily.' This is security-conscious guidance about credential handling, not reconnaissance activity.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Command Execution via p11 CLI
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill executes p11 CLI commands for document management operations (history lookup, comment fetching, document sharing, documentation retrieval). These are legitimate platform-specific commands for the p11 document system. All arguments are defined in the skill instructions, reducing command injection risk. The commands are necessary for the skill's intended function.

content.user_title: Revise P11 Documents from Reviewer Comments
content.value_statement: Reviewing p11 documents with multiple collaborators creates scattered feedback that is hard to apply manually. This skill reads reviewer comments, classifies them by clarity, applies clear decisions to the source document, and publishes a new version automatically.
actual_capabilities: Fetch and classify reviewer comments from p11 documents by clarity and actionability
Apply clear reviewer decisions directly to the source document
Publish revised document versions using the p11 CLI share command
Flag unclear comments that need human clarification before publishing
Reference p11 component library documentation during document editing
Identify the target document from URLs, IDs, or conversation context
limitations: Cannot resolve comment threads that need human negotiation or clarification
Only works with p11 documents, not other document or file formats
Requires the p11 CLI to be installed and available in the environment
Cannot add interactive UI components such as buttons or forms to documents

use_cases.title: Update a Design Proposal After Team Review
use_cases.description: A designer receives feedback on a feature proposal document. The skill applies all clear decisions from reviewer comments and publishes the updated version for final approval.

use_cases.title: Apply Technical Review Feedback to Documentation
use_cases.description: An engineer collects review comments on technical documentation. The skill processes each comment, makes the requested changes, and shares the revised document.

use_cases.title: Batch Process Multiple Rounds of Document Review
use_cases.description: A team lead manages iterative document reviews across multiple rounds. The skill tracks which comments were applied and which still need discussion.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Document Revision
prompt_templates.prompt: Use p11:revise to update the most recent p11 document with reviewer feedback. Fetch the comments and apply all clear decisions.
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time user applying feedback to a recently created document

prompt_templates.title: Revision from a Specific Version
prompt_templates.prompt: Revise p11 document at edit URL [url] from version [number]. Fetch comments from that version and apply clear decisions only.
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs to revise a specific historical version of a document

prompt_templates.title: Handling Unclear or Disputed Comments
prompt_templates.prompt: Use p11:revise on the current document. Classify all visible comment threads. If any need clarification, ask me how to proceed before publishing.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to handle ambiguous feedback with human oversight

prompt_templates.title: Full Document Review with Component Reference
prompt_templates.prompt: Revise the document at [url]. First read the components reference, then fetch all comments. Apply clear decisions and publish. Report which changes were made and which need my input.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user managing a complex multi-thread document review

output_examples.input: Apply the feedback on my design proposal document where the team asked me to add a summary section and reorder the requirements list.
output_examples.output: I reviewed the 8 comment threads on your design proposal. I found 6 clear decisions and 2 that need your input. I applied all clear changes: added the summary section after the introduction, reordered the requirements list by priority, and updated the timeline table. Here is the updated Read URL and Edit URL.

output_examples.input: Update the API documentation with the review comments from version 3.
output_examples.output: I fetched version 3 of the API documentation and found 5 comment threads. All comments had clear decisions, so I applied all of them: corrected the endpoint URLs in the authentication section, added the new rate limit parameters table, and fixed the response format examples. The revised document is published at the new Read URL.

best_practices: Always classify comment threads by content before making changes. Not all unresolved comments require action.
Confirm with the user before publishing when any comment threads need human clarification.
Read the p11 components reference before editing to ensure only supported document components are used.
anti_patterns: Silently ignoring visible comment threads that need human clarification. Always ask the user how to proceed.
Publishing revisions without first fetching and reviewing all comments on the target document.
Adding interactive UI elements like buttons or forms to documents. Use only document-safe components from the p11 library.

faq.question: What types of documents can this skill revise?
faq.answer: This skill works with p11 shareable documents. These are React-based documents created with the p11 platform.

faq.question: Does the skill handle all reviewer comments automatically?
faq.answer: No. The skill applies clear decisions automatically but asks for your input when comments need clarification or are disputed.

faq.question: What happens to comments the skill cannot understand?
faq.answer: Comments that need clarification are flagged. The skill asks whether you want to reply for more information or skip them for now.

faq.question: Do I need to install anything to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. The p11 CLI must be installed globally. The skill can install it with npm install -g @p11-core/cli@latest if needed.

faq.question: Can I revise a specific version of a document?
faq.answer: Yes. You can provide a version number to revise any historical version of a p11 document.

faq.question: How does the skill know which document to revise?
faq.answer: It uses the document URL or ID you provide. If you do not specify one, it uses the most recent p11 document from the conversation.
